senses.gloss: free of, devoid of, deprived of (with عَن (ʕan) or مِن (min))

examples.text:
Abu al-Aswad al-Du'ali
لَا تَنْهَ عَنْ خُلُقٍ وَتَأْتِيَ مِثْلَهُ
عَارٌ عَلَيْكَ إِذَا فَعَلْتَ عَظِيمُ
lā tanha ʕan ḵuluqin wataʔtiya miṯlahu
ʕārun ʕalayka ʔiḏā faʕalta ʕaẓīmu
Do not forbid a thing you do;
examples.english: If you do, shame on you.

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of عَرِيَ (ʕariya, “to become naked”)

Morphologically from the root ع ي ر (ʕ y r).
